The last of the USMC AV-8B Harriers were retired today. I haven't seen one since 2017, so here is a picture of the era over Lake Michigan at the Chicago Air and Water Show. Did you get the chance to see the Harrier fly? If not, there are still Spanish and Italian Harriers in service.
Field sparrow this morning by Lake Michigan near the Sydney Marovitz Golf Course in Chicago. 🪶
Over the weekend, hundreds of curious Chicagoans ventured to Lake Michigan beachfronts to witness a birding marvel: the early arrival of snowy owls.

Kicked off my Birds of the Great Lakes Book Tour at Call & Response Books in Chicago! 🐦📚 Great convo with Karlos from Chicago BIPOC Birders + awesome crowd. Huge thanks to Courtney for the hospitality. You ready? Let’s GO!!! 💪🏾 #BirdJoy #WiscoBirder 🪶

We still have 3ft of snow in the woods. It's maple tapping time, not apple blossom time. Still--here's a barred owl in the apple tree where it used to hang out near my old cabin in the woods (where we tapped maples, as we still do here on Lake Superior). #reductionprint #linocut #owls #mapletapping

On one visit to Amherst Island in Ontario, Canada, the Snowy #Owls we sighted were occupying the east end of the island. Amherst Island lies in Lake Ontario west of Kingston & 3 kilometres offshore, you reach it via a ferry. frametoframe.ca/snowy-owls-o... #birds #birding #nature #birdphotography
A sanderling foraging on the shoreline of Lake Michigan, at Montrose Beach. 🪶

In January, wildlife photographer Julie Maggert snapped a pic of an orange tinted snowy owl near Lake Huron. The photo has since gone viral. Denver Holt, Director of owl research institute said they had never seen any plumage like it in its 35 years of study.

Snowy Owl near Lake Michigan... This beautiful bird appeared very healthy as I observed it in the wild, but now I see a wound on it's left wing in my photo. I hope it makes its way back to the arctic...

Coming from the southwest (US), I did not realize I could see snowy owls around the Great Lakes until I saw this beauty. Since they often hunt during daylight hours, she was easier to see than many other types of owls. 🪶 #snowy #owl #birds #photography #nature #wildlife #protectnature #animals
Juvenile spotted sandpiper seen on the same morning #bird #sandpiper #chicago #lakemichigan #wildlifephotography

Looks like a good winter ahead for Snowy Owl sightings. They've started showing up in rapidly increasing numbers, especially in the western Great Lakes around southern Ontario, Minnesota, Michigan and Wisconsin. www.projectsnowstorm.org/posts/in-wit...

The peregrine falcon is back outside my window! 😮 That’s the Sears Tower and some fog rolling in from Lake Michigan in the background. #Chicago
Got up early this morning to see the snowy owls at Montrose Point. No keepers of them yet (I'll be back), but the sunrise was beautiful. #chicago #lakemichigan #sunrise
Looks like a pair of Black Scoters, female on the right and male on the left! They’re cool sea ducks (although they commonly show up in the Great Lakes in the winter, too).

What a beautiful story about Annabell Whelan, an employee at the Lake Superior Zoo in Duluth, Minnesota rescuing two owls - a great gray owl & a snowy owl in one day. by John Lauritsen WCCO News #kindness #owls #nature #DuluthMN #LakeSuperiorZoo www.cbsnews.com/minnesota/vi...

This was a very unexpected sight at Indian Boundary Park. Two English Carrier Pidgeons, a domesticated breed decended from rock doves. I ended up getting in touch with a volunteer with Great Lakes Pidgeon Rescue, who was able to come collect them this morning.
#birds #Chicago #Pidgeons
Seagulls in the snow, sitting on frozen Lake Michigan waves. This is in Rogers Park, Chicago.
Fun fact, birds along the shores of Lake Michigan in Chicago are dying. We know someone who keeps chickens in a coop about 75 yards from the Lake.

After the woods got too dark for me too safely see any of the many coyotes I have heard here b4, I headed out across the bike path to the beach. The waves were slow, lapping gently. Geese and ducks called out beyond the new parkland west of the beach. More geese flew in, noisily passing the moon.

A friendly snowy owl landed on the Great Lakes freighter James R. Barker on Monday, much to the crew’s delight. See the super cute photos and hear from Sharon Stiteler, also known as the Birdchick, talk about the species’ migration to Minnesota on Morning Edition.

Here's a snowy owl hitching a ride on the Great Lakes freighter James R. Barker in Lake Superior.

Owls are so sweet. I had a special one in the tree by my cabin in the woods over Lake Superior ❤️

Nature is always speaking🌳 A snowy owl hunting along Lake Superior isn’t just survival, it’s a reminder: nourish what matters. Care for people. Feed your purpose. Take that cue, show up w/intention, offer something good, even if it’s just a kind word.🌿🦉 #MarkLangenfeld #TheWitchingHour #shorts
It most certainly is... and we get a few from the far north that spend their winters in our area. Not that owls respect borders, but I guess the hunting is better on this side of Lake Ontario.
There's a flock of geese out there, just swimming along. #lakemichigan #rogerspark #Chicago

This was also a really large bird, probably around 2.5-3 feet tall. Most eagles will take fresh roadkill as a meal and I have even seen owls and eagles drop prey on roads to stun or kill them. Also, I just published the Great Lakes Life feed if you are interested.

And thus is born the urban legend* of the Chicagoose: A pair of mysterious birds who fly in tandem onto your windowsill, and protect Chicago, but should they ever face each other the city would fall into Lake Michigan. *scholars speculate this is a variant of the UK folktale of the Liver Birds.
Gulls hanging out at the beach. Lake Michigan at Rocky Gap Park.

Last night, after midnight I’m sure, I hear a repetitive: pip pip pip pip, and an occasional, not full throated trill

Alt text for above image a photo quickly taken thru a glass window showing a snowy field in a walled off future development area. A lone coyote appears small in the center of the field. Gravel hills for the construction purposes sit in the background and then in the far back is Lake Ontario.
